steps.title: Set y = 0 and isolate the exponential
Count solutions of the exponential equation
steps.content:
Because $a \gt 4$, the base $\frac{a}{4} \gt 1$, so $\left(\frac{a}{4}\right)^{x+c}$ is a strictly increasing, continuous function of $x$ with range $(0, \infty)$. Since $b \gt 0$, the target $\frac{b}{7}$ is a positive real number, which lies in $(0, \infty)$. A strictly increasing function hits each value in its range exactly once, so there is exactly one solution. (The condition $b \gt c$ plays no role in the count.)

The result matches.
